Matches the exact remaining fuel capacity given in setup_task.sh and used as the max_fuel limit in verifier.py.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically modified parameters loosely based on GPS SVN-72, generated dynamically by setup_task.sh. Verified via orbital mechanics calculations that a Hohmann transfer from 26559.7 km to 27059.7 km requires a delta-v of ~36.0 m/s and consumes ~27.8 kg of fuel for a spacecraft with 1682 kg initial mass (1630 kg dry + 52 kg fuel) and 220s Isp. These values explicitly confirm the task.json metadata bounds (20-100 m/s delta-v and 10-52 kg fuel). Additionally, I discovered that the directive falsely states the dry mass of GPS SVN-72 is 1630 kg (which is actually its launch mass; true dry mass is 1136 kg) and falsely assigns PRN 10 to SVN-72 (SVN-73 is PRN 10).
